prompt: A hyper-energetic Japanese shonen manga magazine cover illustration, packed edge to edge with explosive pop-art chaos and bright candy colors, in the style of a weekly comic anthology cover. The main focus is a trendy gyaru teenage girl at the center, leaning toward the viewer in a dynamic foreshortened pose, grinning widely with her mouth open and pointing directly at the camera with one hand while holding a pink toy hammer over her shoulder with the other. She has layered medium-length wavy hair in light brown with blonde and pink streaks, warm amber eyes, multiple colorful hair clips including stars and lightning bolts, earrings, rings, bracelets, necklaces, flashy nail art, and a bold street-fashion outfit: vivid pink satin bomber jacket, white cropped top, black belt with gold buckle, and mixed glam accessories. The cover logo dominates the top center in huge red Japanese lettering with thick black outline and yellow border reading バクアラ!?, with smaller text above it reading 週刊少年. At the upper left, add a loud burst caption reading 超豪華付録(ゴミ)付き!!! with a shocked cartoon trash can mascot. Along the left side place two giant sound-effect style callouts, ドーン! near the top left and バンバーン! in blue near the lower left. On the right side, create an oversized main headline in chunky black, yellow, white, and pink Japanese display type reading 笑って暴れて今日もカオス全開!!, surrounded by smaller promotional blurbs such as 全ページ抱腹絶倒!, 笑いすぎ注意報発令中ーっ!!, and 笑撃の新連載!. In the top right corner include a magazine issue box with the date 2026年4月22日号 and price 390円. Scatter exactly 3 supporting manga-style characters around the central girl: 1 silver-haired boy with goggles on the left aiming a spiral blaster toward the viewer, 1 cheerful purple-haired girl below him holding a dessert drink, and 1 orange-haired idol-like girl in headphones at the upper right making a peace sign. Fill the background and foreground with a dense collage of floating sweets, snacks, toys, doodles, and mascot faces, including donuts, cookies, chocolate bars, candy, pudding, ice cream, popcorn, planets, stars, speech bursts, and goofy creatures, all layered with thick outlines and halftone texture. Along the bottom, include exactly 7 small serialized feature boxes in a row, each with manga-style character art and Japanese titles, creating the look of a crowded anthology teaser strip. Use an ultra-saturated palette of hot pink, yellow, orange, red, cyan, and black, with maximal visual density, overlapping typography, comic speed lines, sticker-like elements, and a glossy printed magazine cover finish. Make it feel hilarious, rebellious, loud, youthful, and irresistibly cluttered, like a parody-yet-premium next-generation Weekly Shonen Jump cover.
